item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The setting itself, and the implications and potential backstory of its discovery or creation are what interests me about this scp. Unfortunately, so such of the article focuses on this person of interest, who, isn't at all interesting to me. I guess I'm just not getting enough of a sense of him as a fleshed out character to relate to him, emotionally.

Something in the exploration log confused me: the POI said he had died 9 times? Are you saying he has been in and out of the salt world, then, 10 times (if you include the exploration with the foundation)? Yet, supposedly he was trapped there? Was he trapped the first time, learned how to leave, then returned over and over, or am I just not putting something together here? I though maybe it implied previous explorations with the Foundation, not listed here, but the dialogue in this one certainly implies it's the first successful attempt at accessing the salt world.

Lastly, I think it merits mention in the containment procedures that some measures are being made to investigate the source of this ritual, since this scp can be accessed with knowledge that was present in book form (published, or hand-written, either way, this knowledge is out in the world for someone to know or discover), and re-created with very accessible materials.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The setting itself, as well as the method of travel, are fairly distinct and interesting, and the exploration got off to a decent start.

I was really dissapointed when it ended up just meandering about for awhile without really accomplishing anything, with a giant shrug of a reveal and a pointless non-resolution for 418.

(By the by, I would take another pass at the part where 418 swings at the agent. The prose suggests the punch was forceful/aggressive, while the dialogue treats it as a playful jab. The combination gives the section an awkward and unatural feeling.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, okay, I feel good upvoting this. I was a little put out by the lack of a big reveal at the end: "it's a simulation" just didn't feel as groundbreaking as it could be. (That, and the final log is way long.) But this has a strong aesthetic all its own. Kind of feels like someone was trying to make heaven for a horse. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't worry, just got it fixed!

They should be fine now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if it's just me, but the pictures keep breaking and then un-breaking every time I refresh. :S

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Alright, I got a cool Series 5 slot! I hope!

But in all seriousness, thank you to the Site19 chat for listening to me tell a story about a thing that happened to me while playing Minecraft and then daring me to write an article about it. Specifically, Modern_Erasmus and RockTeethMothEyes.

And thank you to everyone who critted!
(Captain Kirby, Croquembouche, Dog Teeth, Ihp, LordStonefish, 9Volt and DarkStuff! If I forgot anyone, I'm really, really sorry :( )

I wasn't particularly going for anything deep or existential here, I was just trying to have a little fun!

Edit: Image sources

– hide block
